title: Nested buildah build
body:
**Description**

Attempting to create a stripped down container with buildah running inside of it. Everything is working- except for the docker login. Because runc and buildah are the only things inside this container does an alternative exist to $(docker login)?

**Problem**

```
STEP 1: FROM <Docker Image>
error creating build container: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://<Docker Image>: Error reading manifest latest in <Docker Image>: unauthorized: authentication required
```

**Container Dockerfile**

```
FROM repo.local/oci/go:latest
LABEL local.docker.cmd="docker run -it --name nested-build --privileged repo.local/oci/build:latest"
ENV GOPATH="/root/.go" \
GOBIN="/root/.go/bin"
RUN printf 'dev-util/bats ~amd64\napp-emulation/skopeo ~amd64\n' >>/etc/portage/package.accept_keywords \
 && printf 'FEATURES="-usersandbox -sandbox"\n' >> /etc/portage/env/nosandbox.conf \
 && printf 'dev-go/go-md2man nosandbox.conf\napp-emulation/skopeo nosandbox.conf' >> /etc/portage/package.env \
 && emerge dev-go/sanitized-anchor-name dev-python/pygpgme dev-libs/libassuan sys-libs/libseccomp app-emulation/skopeo sys-fs/fuse sys-libs/libselinux app-admin/sudo dev-go/go-md2man \
 && chmod -R 777 /usr/lib/go/pkg/linux_amd64/* \
 && emerge app-emulation/runc \
 && git clone https://git.local/ostree.git /tmp/ostree-git/ \
 && /tmp/ostree-git/autogen.sh \
 && cd /tmp/ostree-git/ \
 && ./configure \
 && make -j$THREADS \
 && make install \
 && ln -s /usr/local/lib64/libostree-1.* /usr/lib64/ \
 && ln -s /usr/local/lib64/pkgconfig/ostree-1.pc /usr/lib64/pkgconfig/ \
 && go get github.com/projectatomic/buildah
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]
```

`$(docker image inspect repo.local/oci/go:latest|jq '.[].Config.Labels."local.docker.cmd"')`

comments.body:
You could use podman instead of buildah and it would have podman login and podman build, if all you are going to do is build using Dockerfiles.

podman build uses all of the code from Buildah.
Awesome.  If  you blog or tweet about this, ping us to tell us where.

np, glad it worked and feel free to raise an issue either in here or the libpod/podman project if you've a problem with either.  We also monitor  #podman and #buildah channels  on freenode in IRC if you want to reach out and hit us up.
